description: WTI crude futures traded below zero during the COVID-19 demand collapse and storage crunch.
Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine; crude prices jumped on fears of supply disruption and sanctions.
OPEC+ announced a 2 million barrels-per-day production-target cut beginning in November 2022.
Saudi Arabia and other OPEC+ producers announced surprise voluntary oil-output cuts of about 1.16 million barrels per day.
OPEC+ extended most deep output cuts into 2025 while outlining a gradual phase-out plan for some voluntary cuts.
Eight OPEC+ countries unexpectedly accelerated plans to unwind output cuts, adding 411,000 barrels per day in May 2025.
The Iranian Revolution disrupted Iranian production and set off a major global supply shock.
The Iran-Iraq War began, widening the conflict across the Gulf and threatening tanker traffic.
The United States began escorting reflagged Kuwaiti tankers during Operation Earnest Will.
The United States attacked Iranian oil platforms in Operation Nimble Archer after a tanker attack escalation.
The United States attacked Iranian naval targets in Operation Praying Mantis.
U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran escalated the conflict and tightened oil supply expectations.
countries: United States / Iran / Israel / Middle East

description: U.S. officials signaled the military was ready to strike Iran's energy infrastructure if ordered.
Oil jumped as ceasefire hopes faded and violence around the Strait of Hormuz flared again.
Israel's strikes on Iran escalated Middle East conflict and lifted Brent on supply-risk fears.
The United States struck Iranian nuclear sites, intensifying the Israel-Iran war and near-term supply risk.
countries: United States / Iran / Israel / Middle East
